prompt: When the classifier reports low confidence in its intent call, should v1 show a distinguishable 'uncertain' state instead of silently picking the higher-probability bucket?
choices: No — pick the higher-probability bucket and rely on the manual-override badge (v1 default)
Yes — surface an 'uncertain, please confirm' state below some confidence threshold

prompt: How should the read-only answer agent's no-writes boundary be enforced?
choices: A distinct execution-kind dispatch path with a hard-coded reduced tool/RPC table (recommended, matches the existing PreToolUse-hook pattern)
A runtime capability-token check on every RPC call (more defense-in-depth, new machinery)

prompt: Does bucket-2 need a global concurrency cap on simultaneous answer-agent runs across comments/docs, or is v1's per-comment-only guard acceptable to start?

Retiring the magic wand drops all comment-driven affordance for work-item description comments (classification/banner/answer-agent are all scoped to design/investigation docs only). Is this coverage regression acceptable for v1?
Reopen-on-abandon (1&3 path) still needs a closed/abandoned terminal signal the poller doesn't emit today (ClosedUnmerged arm is a no-op). How should v1 get it?
choices: Depend on the chore-lifecycle-pr-closed-unmerged work landing and hook its transition
Add a minimal comment-only reopen hook in the poller's ClosedUnmerged arm now (stopgap)
Ship v1 with resolve-on-merge only; reopen-on-abandon is a fast-follow

prompt: Should one [Revise] click address ALL currently-unaddressed directive/larger-change comments as a single batch (yes), or should v1 let the reviewer select a subset (no)?
